title: 7 tips for using Antigravity 2.0 on enterprise codebases, coding phase
summary: Martin Omander demonstrates seven practical tactics for deploying Antigravity 2.0, an AI coding agent, on enterprise codebases in a production context. The tactics cover agent skill encoding, Git-backed implementation plans, automated test-and-fix loops, visual debugging, async task offloading, architecture visualization, and pre-review verification to reduce human review burden.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech video demonstrates seven practical tactics for running Antigravity 2.0, an AI coding agent, against enterprise codebases during the coding phase. The tips include encoding team practices into agent skills, automated test-driven self-correction, validating UI through headless browsers, and offloading work via asynchronous background tasks. The throughline is keeping an autonomous coding agent reliable on large, real-world repositories rather than toy examples, with closed-loop planning and refactoring as recurring concerns.
